title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Execution Trace: Downgrade Mixed Content
summary: An internal control-plane client uses private roots and optional mTLS through an audited proxy without disabling ordinary certificate validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go downgrade mixed content increment in a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject unintended HTTP downgrades, insecure redirects, mixed content, and credential forwarding across weaker channels. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Model forward proxies, CONNECT tunnels, TLS interception, and mutual TLS credentials as separate authority boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
